perturbation.original_text: For […***…] ([…***…]) years from and after the date of this Agreement, the Company will hold and treat in confidence, and will not use, and will cause their Affiliates to hold and treat in confidence, all non-public documents and information (including any information with regard to terms and conditions of this Agreement) concerning Acquiror and each of its respective Affiliates, except to the extent that such documents and information (1) are required or requested (with prompt notice of such request to be made to Acquiror) to be disclosed by applicable Law or any Governmental Authority, (2) generally become available to the public through no fault of the Company, (3) become available to the Company on a non-confidential basis, or (4) are independently developed by the Company or its Affiliates without reference to the furnished information.
perturbation.changed_text: For […***…] ([…***…]) years from and after the date of this Agreement, the Company will hold and treat in confidence, and will not use, and will cause their Affiliates to hold and treat in confidence, all non-public documents and information (including any information with regard to terms and conditions of this Agreement) concerning Acquiror and each of its respective Affiliates, and must disclose every single non-public documents and information (including any information with regard to terms and conditions of this Agreement) regardless of the source or condition of the information, to any third party that requests it.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding confidentiality. The original text states that the Company will hold certain information in confidence, with specific exceptions. The modified text then mandates disclosure of *all* non-public information to *any* requesting third party, effectively negating the confidentiality provision. Specifically, by mandating disclosure of *all* non-public information, it directly contradicts the exceptions listed in the original text regarding legally required disclosures, publicly available information, and independently developed information. This could lead to uncertainty and legal disputes over what information the Company is obligated to keep confidential and what it is obligated to disclose. The original text specifies exceptions to what can be disclosed, while the changed text says all Non-public information must be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For […***…] ([…***…]) years from and after the date of this Agreement, the Company will hold and treat in confidence, and will not use, and will cause their Affiliates to hold and treat in confidence, all non-public documents and information (including any information with regard to terms and conditions of this Agreement) concerning Acquiror and each of its respective Affiliates, and must disclose every single non-public documents and information (including any information with regard to terms and conditions of this Agreement) regardless of the source or condition of the information, to any third party that requests it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text outlines specific exceptions to the confidentiality of Acquiror's non-public information, such as legally required disclosures and publicly available information. However, the modified text mandates the disclosure of *all* non-public information to *any* requesting third party, regardless of the source or condition of the information. This directly negates the previously stated exceptions and creates a clear contradiction. The contradicted text further emphasizes the company's responsibility to disclose every single non-public document, which stands in direct contradiction to the exceptions laid out previously in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Employees of Company. Following the Closing Date, SPI shall develop an integration plan in consultation with the management of the Company as required for combining the business operations of the two companies. Subject to the goals, parameters and integration activities outlined in the integration plan, SPI shall (i) provide the employees of the Company with employee incentives under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications) to the incentives made available by the Company to such its employees as of the date of this Agreement […***…], and thereafter under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications, local laws, and SPI's practices with respect to the employees of Acquiror) to those of the incentives made available by SPI to its employees, and (ii) […***…] the […***…] of the […***… ] of the […***…] as of the […***…] of this [… ***…] at […***…] the […***…] of the […***…] of the […***…] on […***…]. For the sake of achieving the purpose of the strategic alliance as set forth in Section 2.01, both Parties acknowledge their mutual intention to, in principle, maintain the Company's employment at levels consistent with the requirements of the Company from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: Employees of Company. Following the Closing Date, SPI shall develop an integration plan in consultation with the management of the Company as required for combining the business operations of the two companies. Subject to the goals, parameters and integration activities outlined in the integration plan, SPI shall (i) provide the employees of the Company with employee incentives under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications) to the incentives made available by the Company to such its employees as of the date of this Agreement […***…], and thereafter under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications, local laws, and SPI's practices with respect to the employees of Acquiror) to those of the incentives made available by SPI to its employees, and (ii) […***…] the […***…] of the […***… ] of the […***…] as of the […***…] of this [… ***…] at […***…] the […***…] of the […***…] of the […***…] on […***…]. For the sake of achieving the purpose of the strategic alliance as set forth in Section 2.01, both Parties acknowledge their mutual intention to, at their own discretion, terminate any existing employment at levels inconsistent with the requirements of the Company from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction in the assurance of employment levels. Initially, it states the intention to 'in principle, maintain the Company's employment at levels consistent with the requirements of the Company from time to time.' The perturbation adds 'at their own discretion, terminate any existing employment at levels inconsistent with the requirements of the Company from time to time,' which allows the acquiring company to terminate employment at will, directly contradicting the previous statement about maintaining employment. This creates uncertainty about job security for employees of the Company, potentially leading to legal challenges or decreased morale.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4.10 Employees of Company. Following the Closing Date, SPI shall develop an integration plan in consultation with the management of the Company as required for combining the business operations of the two companies. Subject to the goals, parameters and integration activities outlined in the integration plan, SPI shall (i) provide the employees of the Company with employee incentives under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications) to the incentives made available by the Company to such its employees as of the date of this Agreement […***…], and thereafter under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications, local laws, and SPI's practices with respect to the employees of Acquiror) to those of the incentives made available by SPI to its employees, and (ii) […***…] the […***…] of the […***… ] of the […***…] as of the […***…] of this [… ***…] at […***…] the […***…] of the […***…] of the […***…] on […***…]. For the sake of achieving the purpose of the strategic alliance as set forth in Section 2.01, both Parties acknowledge their mutual intention to, at their own discretion, terminate any existing employment at levels inconsistent with the requirements of the Company from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The original text indicates a mutual intention to maintain employment levels. The changed text introduces a clause allowing for termination of employment at will, directly contradicting the initial intention to maintain employment levels. The contradiction is strong because it fundamentally alters the job security expectations, rendering the original intention meaningless. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, making it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be terminated after the end of the Offer Period if the Offer is successful.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated after the end of the Offer Period regardless if the Offer is successful or not, as long as both parties come to agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'This Agreement may not be terminated after the end of the Offer Period if the Offer is successful.' This implies that once the offer is successful and the offer period ends, termination is impossible. The altered text reads 'This Agreement may be terminated after the end of the Offer Period regardless if the Offer is successful or not, as long as both parties come to agreement.' Now the agreement can be terminated after the offer period ends, as long as both parties agree to terminate it, contradicting what the original agreement was stating. The added text gives the ability for the agreement to be broken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated after the end of the Offer Period regardless if the Offer is successful or not, as long as both parties come to agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement could not be terminated after the offer period if the offer was successful. The changed text allows termination after the offer period regardless of the offer's success, as long as both parties agree. This directly contradicts the original text's prohibition of termination in the specific scenario of a successful offer after the offer period. The contradiction exists because the changed text provides a condition (mutual agreement) that overrides the original text's absolute restriction, making the original section regarding termination unenforceable. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
